question: If (a + b + c) = 12 and (a² + b² + c²) = 60, find the value of (a − b)² + (b − c)² + (c − a)².
option_1: 45
option_2: 28
option_3: 54
option_4: 36
solution_text:
(a − b)² + (b − c)² + (c − a)²
= 2(a² + b² + c² − ab − bc − ca)

(a + b + c)² = a² + b² + c² + 2(ab + bc + ca)
144 = 60 + 2(ab + bc + ca)
2(ab + bc + ca) = 84
ab + bc + ca = 42
now,
(a − b)² + (b − c)² + (c − a)² = 2(60 − 42)
= 2 × 18
= 36

question: The radius of base of a solid cylinder is 12 cm and its height is 15 cm. It is melted and converted into small bullets. Each bullet is of same size. Each bullet consisted of two parts viz. a cylinder and a hemisphere on one of its base. The total height of bullet is 5 cm and radius of base is 3 cm. How many complete bullets can be obtained?
option_1: 59
option_2: 60
option_3: 61
option_4: 62
solution_text:
Volume of the larger cylinder = πr²h = π × 12² × 15 = 2160π

Bullet = Cylinder + Hemisphere

Radius = 3 cm, Total height = 5 cm

Height of cylindrical part = 5 − 3 = 2 cm

Volume of cylinder = π × 3² × 2 = 18π

Volume of hemisphere = (2/3)π × 3³ = (2/3)π × 27 = 18π

Volume of one bullet = 18π + 18π = 36π

Number of bullets = 2160π / 36π = 60

question: If 27x³ + y³ = 54 and 3x + y = 6, then find the value of 81x⁴ + y⁴.
option_1: 162
option_2: 132
option_3: 184
option_4: 146
solution_text:
(3x)³ + y³ = 54, 3x + y = 6

⇒ 54 = 6(9x² − 3xy + y²)
⇒ 9x² − 3xy + y² = 9 ...(1)

(3x + y)² = 36
⇒ 9x² + 6xy + y² = 36 ...(2)

(2) − (1):
9xy = 27 ⇒ xy = 3

From (1):
9x² + y² = 9 + 3xy = 18

81x⁴ + y⁴ = (9x² + y²)² − 2·9x²y²
= 18² − 18·9
= 324 − 162
= 162

question:
Read the following paragraph and answer the given questions accordingly:

The 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami was triggered by a massive undersea earthquake off the coast of Sumatra, registering a staggering magnitude. As the tectonic plates shifted violently, they displaced billions of tons of water, sending concentric ripples across the deep ocean at the speed of a jet aircraft. In the open sea, these waves remained deceptive—mere swells only a few feet high—allowing unsuspecting mariners to sail over them without realizing the impending doom racing toward the distant coastlines.

However, as the energy reached the shallower coastal shelves, the phenomenon of "shoaling" occurred. The front of the wave slowed down due to friction with the seabed, while the back continued at high speed, forcing the water to wall up into terrifying heights. Eyewitnesses in Thailand and India described the initial retreat of the shoreline as a "hollowed-out" sea, luring curious onlookers toward the exposed seabed just moments before the crest slammed inland. The aftermath was a somber testament to nature’s caprice, leaving behind a trail of decimated infrastructure and shifted topographies.

Q- According to the passage, why were mariners in the open sea unable to detect the tsunami?
option_1: The waves were traveling at a very slow speed.
option_2: The height of the waves was relatively minimal.
option_3: The tectonic plates had not yet shifted fully.
option_4: The water displacement was not significant yet.
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (b) The height of the waves was relatively minimal.

Explanation:

  • The passage clearly states that in the open sea, waves were “mere swells only a few feet high”
  • Because of this low height, mariners could not recognise the tsunami
  • The waves were actually moving very fast, so speed was not the issue

Key Point:

  • Deep ocean → low wave height + high speed → difficult to detect tsunami

Q- What does the term "shoaling" refer to in the second paragraph?
option_1: The process of tectonic plates sliding under each other.
option_2: The deceptive calm observed in the middle of the ocean.
option_3: The sudden retreat of water from the coastal shoreline.
option_4: The increase in wave height as it reaches shallow water.
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (d) The increase in wave height as it reaches shallow water.

Explanation:

  • Passage says: when waves reach shallow coastal areas, the front slows due to friction while the back continues fast
  • This causes water to pile up and form huge waves
  • This process is called “shoaling”

Why others are wrong:

  • (a) related to tectonic movement, not shoaling
  • (b) refers to open sea calm, not shoaling
  • (c) retreat of water is an effect, not definition

Key Point:

  • Shoaling = increase in wave height in shallow water due to slowing of wave front

question:
Change the narration of the following sentence:

He said, "I have been working since morning."
option_1: He said that he had been working since morning.
option_2: He said that he has been working since morning.
option_3: He said that he was working since morning.
option_4: He said that he had worked since morning.
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (a) He said that he had been working since morning.

Explanation:

  • Direct speech tense: Present Perfect Continuous (have been working)
  • Indirect speech → Past Perfect Continuous (had been working)
  • “I” → “he” (pronoun change)

Why others are wrong:

  • (b) has been working → no tense change
  • (c) was working → wrong tense (continuous but not perfect)
  • (d) had worked → changes meaning (loses continuity)

Key Rule:

  • Present Perfect Continuous → Past Perfect Continuous in indirect speech

question:
Identify the error given in the sentence:

I ordered / some books on Hindi Grammar / but no one / has arrived yet.
option_1: I ordered
option_2: some books on Hindi Grammar
option_3: but no one
option_4: has arrived yet.
solution_text:
The sentence is: I ordered / some books on Hindi Grammar / but no one / has arrived yet.

Error Identification:

The error is in “but no one”.

  • “No one” refers to a person.
  • Here, the subject is books, so the correct word should be “none” (meaning “not any”).

Corrected Sentence:

I ordered some books on Hindi Grammar but none has arrived yet.

Explanation:

  • I ordered → Correct, shows the action.
  • some books on Hindi Grammar → Correct, specifies the object.
  • but no one → Incorrect, because “no one” means “no person,” while the sentence is about books.
  • has arrived yet → Correct, indicates the books have not come.

Option Meanings:

  1. Rude (असभ्य / बदतमीज़) → Synonym, not antonym.
  2. Rough (कठोर / असभ्य) → Synonym, not antonym.
  3. Boorish (गंवार / असभ्य) → Synonym, not antonym.
  4. Cultured (सभ्य / सुसंस्कृत) → ✅ Antonym, opposite of uncouth.

question:
Fill in the Blank:

He is very ________; he can be easily tricked by anyone.
option_1: Credible
option_2: Creditable
option_3: Credulous
option_4: Credential
solution_text:
The sentence is: He is very ________; he can be easily tricked by anyone.

👉 The correct word is Credulous (भोला / आसानी से विश्वास करने वाला).

✅ Correct Answer: Credulous (भोला / आसानी से विश्वास करने वाला)

Option Meanings:

  1. Credible (विश्वसनीय / भरोसेमंद) → ❌ Means believable, not easily tricked.
  2. Creditable (प्रशंसनीय / सराहनीय) → ❌ Means deserving praise, not related to being tricked.
  3. Credulous (भोला / आसानी से विश्वास करने वाला) → ✅ Correct, someone who is easily deceived.
  4. Credential (प्रमाणपत्र / योग्यता प्रमाण) → ❌ Means qualification or certificate, not a personality trait.

question:
Identify the error given in the sentence:

Mr. Sharma has / driven almost twice / so fast as / Mr. Verma has.
option_1: Mr. Sharma has
option_2: driven almost twice
option_3: so fast as
option_4: Mr. Verma has.
solution_text:
The sentence is: Mr. Sharma has / driven almost twice / so fast as / Mr. Verma has.

Error Identification:

The error is in “so fast as”.

  • The correct comparative structure is “as fast as”, not “so fast as.”
  • “So…as” is used in negative comparisons (e.g., He is not so tall as his brother).
  • Since this is a positive comparison, it must be “as fast as.”

Corrected Sentence:

Mr. Sharma has driven almost twice as fast as Mr. Verma has.

question:
One Word Substitution:

A story in which animals or objects speak to give a moral lesson.
option_1: Emissary
option_2: Parable
option_3: Allegory
option_4: Fable
solution_text:
The correct one-word substitution for “A story in which animals or objects speak to give a moral lesson” is Fable (नीतिकथा / पशु-पक्षियों या वस्तुओं की बोलने वाली कहानी).

✅ Correct Answer: Fable (नीतिकथा)

Option Meanings:

  1. Emissary (दूत / प्रतिनिधि) → ❌ Incorrect, means a messenger or representative.
  2. Parable (दृष्टांत / उपदेशात्मक कहानी) → ❌ Close, but usually involves human characters to teach a moral lesson.
  3. Allegory (रूपक कथा / प्रतीकात्मक कहानी) → ❌ Symbolic story with deeper meaning, not specifically animals/objects speaking.
  4. Fable (नीतिकथा / पशु-पक्षियों या वस्तुओं की बोलने वाली कहानी) → ✅ Correct, short story where animals/objects speak and convey a moral.

question:
Change the voice of the following sentence:

You can see the peaks of the Himalayas from this point.
option_1: The peaks of the Himalayas could be seen from this point.
option_2: The peaks of the Himalayas can seen from this point.
option_3: The peaks of the Himalayas can be seen from this point.
option_4: From this point, the peaks of the Himalayas are seen.
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (c) The peaks of the Himalayas can be seen from this point.

Explanation:

  • Active sentence: You can see… (modal can)
  • Passive structure: can + be + V3 (seen)
  • Object “the peaks of the Himalayas” becomes subject

Why others are wrong:

  • (a) could be seen → tense changed (wrong)
  • (b) can seen → missing “be”
  • (d) are seen → modal removed (wrong)

Key Rule:

  • Modal (can/may/must) → modal + be + V3 (Passive)

question:
IDIOM:

To flog a dead horse
option_1: To be cruel to animals
option_2: To waste energy
option_3: To be a jockey
option_4: To work hard
solution_text:
The idiom “To flog a dead horse” means to waste energy on something pointless or futile (बेकार काम पर ऊर्जा खर्च करना / व्यर्थ प्रयास करना).

✅ Correct Answer: To waste energy (व्यर्थ ऊर्जा खर्च करना)

Option Meanings:

  1. To be cruel to animals (जानवरों के प्रति क्रूर होना) → ❌ Literal, not idiomatic.
  2. To waste energy (व्यर्थ ऊर्जा खर्च करना) → ✅ Correct meaning.
  3. To be a jockey (घुड़सवार होना) → ❌ Irrelevant.
  4. To work hard (कड़ी मेहनत करना) → ❌ Incorrect, the idiom is about futile effort, not genuine hard work.

question:
Identify the error given in the sentence:

The master / did not know / who of the servants / broke the vase.
option_1: The master
option_2: did not know
option_3: who of the servants
option_4: broke the vase.
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (c) who of the servants

Explanation:

  • Correct usage is “which of the servants”, not “who of the servants”
  • “Who” is used alone, but when selecting from a group → use “which of”
  • Correct sentence:
    “The master did not know which of the servants broke the vase.”

Why others are correct:

  • (a) “The master” → correct subject
  • (b) “did not know” → correct verb phrase
  • (d) “broke the vase” → correct past tense

Key Rule:

  • Which of + group → correct
  • Who of + group → incorrect

question:
One Word Substitution:

One who is honourably discharged from service.
option_1: Emeritus
option_2: Mercenary
option_3: Sojourn
option_4: Bellicose
solution_text:
The correct one-word substitution for “One who is honourably discharged from service” is Emeritus (सम्मानपूर्वक सेवा से मुक्त व्यक्ति / मानद पदवीधारी).

✅ Correct Answer: Emeritus (सम्मानपूर्वक सेवा से मुक्त व्यक्ति)

Option Meanings:

  1. Emeritus (मानद पदवीधारी / सम्मानपूर्वक सेवा से मुक्त) → ✅ Correct, used for someone retired but retaining honorary title.
  2. Mercenary (भाड़े का सैनिक / स्वार्थी व्यक्ति) → ❌ Incorrect, refers to one who works for money, especially in military.
  3. Sojourn (अल्पकालीन प्रवास) → ❌ Incorrect, means a temporary stay.
  4. Bellicose (युद्धप्रिय / लड़ाकू स्वभाव वाला) → ❌ Incorrect, means aggressive or warlike.

question:
Arrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph:

P: Therefore, the committee recommended a "cradle-to-grave" approach for electronic waste disposal.

Q: The sheer volume of discarded smartphones and laptops has created a global environmental crisis.

R: Many of these devices contain toxic heavy metals like lead and mercury that seep into the soil.

S: Informal recycling sectors in developing countries often expose workers to these dangerous chemicals.
option_1: SPQR
option_2: QSPR
option_3: SRPQ
option_4: QRSP
solution_text:
Correct Order: Q → R → S → P

Explanation:

  • Q introduces the problem (e-waste crisis)
  • R explains the cause (toxic metals in devices)
  • S adds impact (workers exposed in informal recycling)
  • P concludes with solution (recommendation)

Final Paragraph:

The sheer volume of discarded smartphones and laptops has created a global environmental crisis. Many of these devices contain toxic heavy metals like lead and mercury that seep into the soil. Informal recycling sectors in developing countries often expose workers to these dangerous chemicals. Therefore, the committee recommended a "cradle-to-grave" approach for electronic waste disposal.

question:
Change the narration of the following sentence:

He said to me, "Do you know the way to the market?"
option_1: He asked me did I know the way to the market.
option_2: He told me if I know the way to the market.
option_3: He asked me that if I knew the way to the market.
option_4: He asked me if I knew the way to the market.
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (d) He asked me if I knew the way to the market.

Explanation (Easy + Exam Focus):

  • Direct speech is a yes/no question → use “if/whether”
  • “said to” → “asked”
  • “Do you know” → “I knew” (present → past, backshift)
  • Sentence becomes statement form (no inversion)

Why others are wrong:

  • (a) did I know → wrong structure (question form used)
  • (b) told me if I know → wrong verb + no tense change
  • (c) that if → incorrect combination

Key Rule:

  • Yes/No question → asked + if/whether + subject + verb (past form)

question: Antonym of UPBRAID
option_1: Scold
option_2: Chide
option_3: Praise
option_4: Rebuke
solution_text:
The word “Upbraid” means to scold, rebuke, or criticize severely (डाँटना / फटकारना). So, its antonym will be Praise (प्रशंसा करना / सराहना करना).

✅ Correct Answer: Praise (प्रशंसा करना)

Option Meanings:

  1. Scold (डाँटना) → Synonym, not antonym.
  2. Chide (फटकारना / डाँटना) → Synonym, not antonym.
  3. Praise (प्रशंसा करना / सराहना करना) → ✅ Antonym, opposite of upbraid.
  4. Rebuke (फटकारना / आलोचना करना) → Synonym, not antonym.

question:
Change the voice of the following sentence:

The audience loudly cheered the leader’s speech.
option_1: The leader’s speech is loudly cheered by the audience.
option_2: The leader’s speech was loudly cheered by the audience.
option_3: The leader’s speech had been loudly cheered by the audience.
option_4: The leader’s speech was being loudly cheered by the audience.
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (b) The leader’s speech was loudly cheered by the audience.

Explanation:

  • Active sentence is in Simple Past (cheered)
  • Passive form → was + V3 (cheered)
  • Object “the leader’s speech” becomes subject

Why others are wrong:

  • (a) is cheered → Present tense (wrong)
  • (c) had been cheered → Past Perfect (wrong)
  • (d) was being cheered → Past Continuous (wrong)

Key Rule:

  • Simple Past Active → was/were + V3 (Passive)

question:
Fill in the Blank:

The ________ of the wood was used for making fire.
option_1: Faggot
option_2: Facet
option_3: Factor
option_4: Faction
solution_text:
The sentence is: The ________ of the wood was used for making fire.

👉 The correct word is Faggot (लकड़ी का गट्ठर / ईंधन के लिए बाँधी गई लकड़ियाँ).

✅ Correct Answer: Faggot (लकड़ी का गट्ठर)

Option Meanings:

  1. Faggot (लकड़ी का गट्ठर / ईंधन के लिए बाँधी गई लकड़ियाँ) → ✅ Correct, bundle of sticks used for firewood.
  2. Facet (पहलू / पक्ष) → ❌ Means aspect or side, not related to wood.
  3. Factor (कारक / कारण) → ❌ Means cause or element, not related to wood.
  4. Faction (गुट / दल) → ❌ Means group or party, not related to wood.

question: Synonym of JETTISON
option_1: To accept
option_2: To keep
option_3: To embrace
option_4: To discard
solution_text:
The word “Jettison” means to throw away, discard, or abandon something (फेंक देना / त्याग देना).

✅ Correct Answer: To discard (त्याग देना / फेंक देना)

Option Meanings:

  1. To accept (स्वीकार करना) → ❌ Opposite meaning.
  2. To keep (रखना / बनाए रखना) → ❌ Opposite meaning.
  3. To embrace (गले लगाना / अपनाना) → ❌ Opposite meaning.
  4. To discard (त्याग देना / फेंक देना) → ✅ Correct synonym of jettison.

question: The 'Nayanars' were a group of 63 saints devoted to which deity in early medieval South India?
option_1: Vishnu
option_2: Shakti
option_3: Shiva
option_4: Kartikeya
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (c) Shiva

Explanation:

  • The Nayanars were a group of 63 Tamil poet-saints who lived in early medieval South India (6th–9th century CE).
  • They were ardent followers of Shaivism, meaning they were devoted to Lord Shiva.
  • They composed devotional hymns in Tamil, many of which are compiled in the sacred text called the Tevaram.
  • Their movement played a major role in the Bhakti Movement, promoting personal devotion over ritualism.

question: 'Operation Flood' is associated with the production of which commodity?
option_1: Petroleum
option_2: Milk
option_3: Edible Oil
option_4: Fish
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Milk

Explanation

Operation Flood was launched in 1970 by the National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) under the leadership of Dr. Verghese Kurien, who is often called the Father of the White Revolution in India.

  • It was the world’s largest dairy development program, aimed at creating a nationwide milk grid.
  • The project made India the largest producer of milk and dairy products in the world.
  • It helped rural farmers by linking them directly to consumers, eliminating middlemen, and ensuring fair prices.
  • The success of Operation Flood transformed India from a milk-deficient country into a self-sufficient and surplus producer of milk.

question: 'Gatka', a traditional martial art form which was included in the National Games, originated from which state?
option_1: Tamil Nadu
option_2: Manipur
option_3: Kerela
option_4: Punjab
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (d) Punjab

Explanation:

  • Gatka is a traditional martial art form associated with the Sikh community.
  • It originated in the state of Punjab and was historically used by Sikh warriors for self-defence and combat training.
  • Gatka involves the use of wooden sticks (soti), swords, and other traditional weapons.
  • It has been recognised and included in events like the National Games to promote India’s traditional sports.

question: 'Eustachian Tube' is a part of the human body located in the:
option_1: Ear
option_2: Nose
option_3: Eye
option_4: Throat
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Ear

Explanation

The Eustachian Tube is a narrow passage that connects the middle ear to the nasopharynx (upper throat and back of the nasal cavity).

  • Its main function is to equalize air pressure on both sides of the eardrum, which is essential for proper hearing.
  • It also helps drain mucus from the middle ear into the throat.
  • When the tube is blocked (e.g., during a cold or sinus infection), it can cause ear pain, hearing problems, or infections.

question: The device used to convert Alternating Current (AC) to Direct Current (DC) is:
option_1: Transformer
option_2: Rectifier
option_3: Inverter
option_4: Generator
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Rectifier

Explanation

  • A Rectifier is the device used to convert Alternating Current (AC) into Direct Current (DC).
  • It works by allowing current to flow only in one direction, effectively eliminating the negative half of the AC cycle.
  • Types of rectifiers include:
    • Half-wave rectifier (uses one diode)
    • Full-wave rectifier (uses two diodes or a bridge of four diodes)
  • Rectifiers are widely used in power supplies for electronic devices, ensuring they receive stable DC voltage.

question: Which of the following is the correct sequence of the Great Himalayas peaks from East to West?
option_1: Nanda Devi – Mt. Everest – Kanchenjunga – Namcha Barwa
option_2: Namcha Barwa – Mt. Everest – Kanchenjunga – Nanda Devi
option_3: Namcha Barwa – Kanchenjunga – Mt. Everest – Nanda Devi
option_4: Nanda Devi – Kanchenjunga – Mt. Everest – Namcha Barwa
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Namcha Barwa – Kanchenjunga – Mt. Everest – Nanda Devi

Explanation

The Great Himalayas stretch from East (Arunachal Pradesh/Tibet region) to West (Uttarakhand region). The correct east-to-west order of the given peaks is:

  1. Namcha Barwa – Located in Tibet near the easternmost bend of the Brahmaputra River.
  2. Kanchenjunga – Situated on the border of Nepal and Sikkim, India.
  3. Mt. Everest – On the Nepal–Tibet border, the highest peak in the world.
  4. Nanda Devi – Located in Uttarakhand, India, the second-highest peak in India.

Thus, the correct sequence is: Namcha Barwa → Kanchenjunga → Mt. Everest → Nanda Devi

question: Which committee in India recommended the use of 'Calorie Intake' (2400 for rural, 2100 for urban) as a measure of the poverty line?
option_1: Tendulkar Committee
option_2: Alagh Committee
option_3: Lakdawala Committee
option_4: Rangarajan Committee
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (b) Alagh Committee

Explanation:

  • The Alagh Committee (1979) was the first to officially define the poverty line based on calorie intake.
  • It recommended 2400 calories per day for rural areas and 2100 calories per day for urban areas.
  • This approach linked poverty with the minimum nutritional requirement needed for survival.
  • Later committees like Lakdawala, Tendulkar, and Rangarajan modified the methodology, but the calorie-based norm originated from the Alagh Committee.

question: The 'Union Public Service Commission' (UPSC) submits its annual report to:
option_1: The Parliament
option_2: The Cabinet headed by Prime Minister
option_3: The President
option_4: The Speaker of Lok Sabha
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (c) The President

Explanation:

  • The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) submits its annual report to the President of India.
  • This provision is mentioned in Article 323 of the Indian Constitution.
  • The President places the report before both Houses of Parliament, along with a memorandum explaining actions taken on the recommendations.
  • Thus, while Parliament discusses it, the report is formally submitted to the President.

question: Which Sultan of Delhi founded the city of Agra in 1504?
option_1: Sikandar Lodi
option_2: Bahlul Lodi
option_3: Ibrahim Lodi
option_4: Khizr Khan
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Sikandar Lodi

Explanation

  • The city of Agra was founded in 1504 by Sultan Sikandar Lodi, the second ruler of the Lodi dynasty.
  • He shifted his capital from Delhi to Agra because of its strategic location on the banks of the Yamuna River, which made it easier to control the fertile Doab region.
  • After Sikandar Lodi’s death, his son Ibrahim Lodi ruled from Agra until he was defeated by Babur in the Battle of Panipat (1526), which marked the beginning of the Mughal Empire.
  • Agra later became one of the most important cities under the Mughals, especially during the reign of Akbar, Jahangir, and Shah Jahan.

question: The 'First Law Officer' of the Government of India is:
option_1: Chief Justice of India
option_2: Cabinet Minister of Law & Justice
option_3: Attorney General for India
option_4: Law Secretary of India
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Attorney General for India

Explanation

  • The Attorney General for India is the First Law Officer of the Government of India.
  • This position is established under Article 76 of the Indian Constitution.
  • The Attorney General:
    • Provides legal advice to the Government of India.
    • Represents the Union Government in the Supreme Court and High Courts.
    • Has the right to participate in parliamentary proceedings (without voting rights).
  • Other roles:
    • Chief Justice of India → Head of the judiciary, not the government’s law officer.
    • Cabinet Minister of Law & Justice → Political head of the ministry, not the first law officer.
    • Law Secretary of India → Administrative officer, not constitutional law officer.

question: The 'Bowman's Capsule' is a functional part of which human organ?
option_1: Heart
option_2: Kidney
option_3: Brain
option_4: Lungs
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Kidney

Explanation

  • The Bowman’s Capsule is a cup-shaped structure located in the nephrons of the kidney.
  • It surrounds the glomerulus, a network of capillaries where blood filtration begins.
  • Its primary function is to collect the filtrate (water, salts, glucose, urea, etc.) that passes out of the blood through the glomerulus.
  • This marks the first step in urine formation.

question: 'Operation Greens' is a government scheme focused on the supply of:
option_1: Green Leafy vegetables
option_2: Organic fertilizers
option_3: Pulses and Cereals
option_4: Tomato, Onion, and Potato
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Tomato, Onion, and Potato

Explanation

  • Operation Greens is a scheme launched by the Government of India in 2018 under the Ministry of Food Processing Industries.
  • Its primary objective is to stabilize the supply and price of TOP crops (Tomato, Onion, Potato) across the country.
  • The scheme aims to:
    • Reduce price volatility of these essential vegetables.
    • Ensure farmers get fair prices and consumers get affordable rates.
    • Promote Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), agri-logistics, processing facilities, and professional management.
  • It is often referred to as the “TOP to Total” initiative, symbolizing its focus on these three crops.

question: India’s first 'Semiconductor Fabrication' plant, which began its trial production in September 2025, is located in which city?
option_1: Gurugram industrial zone
option_2: Dholera industrial zone
option_3: Hyderabad industrial zone
option_4: Bengaluru industrial zone
solution_text:
India’s first semiconductor fabrication plant began trial production in September 2025 at the Dholera Special Investment Region (SIR), Gujarat. This facility, led by Tata Electronics in partnership with Taiwan’s Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation (PSMC), marks a historic milestone in India’s semiconductor mission.

Key Details

  • Location: Dholera Industrial Zone (Dholera Smart City, Gujarat)
  • Developer: Tata Electronics in collaboration with PSMC (Taiwan)
  • Investment: Approx. ₹91,000 crore (₹910 billion)
  • Timeline: Trial production began in September 2025
  • Significance:
    • First large-scale semiconductor fabrication (chip-making) plant in India.
    • Positioned within the Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor for strategic connectivity.
    • Supports India’s Semiconductor Mission (ISM) to reduce dependence on imports.

Why Dholera Was Chosen

  • Strategic Location: Close to major ports and industrial hubs.
  • Infrastructure: Dholera Smart City offers advanced industrial infrastructure and reliable power supply.
  • Government Support: Strong incentives under the India Semiconductor Mission.

question: The 'Council of States' (Rajya Sabha) was first constituted in which year?
option_1: 1948
option_2: 1950
option_3: 1952
option_4: 1954
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: 1952

Explanation

  • The Council of States (Rajya Sabha) is the upper house of the Indian Parliament.
  • Though the Constitution of India came into effect in 1950, the first elections to the Rajya Sabha were held in 1952, and it was formally constituted that year.
  • The Rajya Sabha is a permanent body and is not subject to dissolution, but one-third of its members retire every two years.

question: The 'Ghatam', a large clay pot used in Carnatic music, is classified under which category of musical instruments?
option_1: Idiophone
option_2: Aerophone
option_3: Chordophone
option_4: Membranophone
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Idiophone

Explanation

  • The Ghatam is a traditional percussion instrument used in Carnatic music.
  • It is essentially a large clay pot that produces sound when struck with the hands, fingers, or even nails.
  • Since the sound is produced by the vibration of the instrument’s body itself (without strings, membranes, or airflow), it is classified as an Idiophone.
  • Idiophones are instruments where sound is generated by the material of the instrument vibrating, examples include xylophone, cymbals, and ghatam.

question: Which Indian leader attended all three Round Table Conferences in London?
option_1: Mahatma Gandhi
option_2: Jawaharlal Nehru
option_3: B.R. Ambedkar
option_4: Sarojini Naidu
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: B.R. Ambedkar

Explanation

The Round Table Conferences were held in London between 1930–1932 to discuss constitutional reforms for India.

  • B.R. Ambedkar was the only Indian leader who attended all three conferences.
  • Mahatma Gandhi attended only the Second Round Table Conference (1931) as the sole representative of the Indian National Congress.
  • Jawaharlal Nehru and Sarojini Naidu participated in some sessions but not all three.
  • Ambedkar used these conferences to highlight the plight of the depressed classes (Dalits) and demanded separate electorates, which later led to the Poona Pact (1932).

question: Which port is known as the 'Queen of the Arabian Sea'?
option_1: Kandla Port
option_2: Mumbai Port
option_3: Mormugao Port
option_4: Kochi Port
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Kochi Port

Explanation

  • Kochi Port, located in Kerala, is famously known as the “Queen of the Arabian Sea.”
  • Historically, Kochi was a major center for the spice trade, attracting traders from Arabia, China, and later Europe.
  • The port’s strategic location made it one of the most important maritime hubs in the Indian Ocean.
  • Even today, Kochi remains a vital port for international trade and naval operations.

question: In which specific state is the 'Bagurumba' folk dance performed by the indigenous Bodo community during festivals?
option_1: Odisha
option_2: Bihar
option_3: Assam
option_4: Sikkim
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Assam

Explanation

  • Bagurumba is the traditional folk dance of the Bodo community, performed mainly in Assam.
  • It is popularly known as the “Butterfly Dance”, as the movements resemble the graceful fluttering of butterflies.
  • The dance is performed by women during the Bwisagu festival (celebrated in spring), showcasing harmony with nature.
  • It reflects the Bodo people's deep connection with their environment, culture, and traditions.

question: 'Laterite Soil' is rich in which of the following?
option_1: Nitrogen and Potash
option_2: Iron Oxide and Aluminium
option_3: Humus and Nitrogen
option_4: Phosphorus and Calcium
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Iron Oxide and Aluminium

Explanation

  • Laterite Soil is formed under high temperature and heavy rainfall conditions, typical of tropical and subtropical regions.
  • It is the result of intense leaching, which removes most soluble minerals like nitrogen, potash, and humus.
  • What remains are oxides of iron and aluminium, giving the soil its characteristic red color.
  • These soils are generally poor in fertility but are suitable for crops like tea, coffee, cashew, and rubber when properly managed.

question: During which Five-Year Plan was the slogan 'Garibi Hatao' (Removal of Poverty) introduced?
option_1: Third Plan
option_2: Fourth Plan
option_3: Fifth Plan
option_4: Sixth Plan
solution_text:
The slogan “Garibi Hatao” (Removal of Poverty) was introduced during the Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974–1979) under Prime Minister Indira Gandhi.

Explanation

  • Garibi Hatao Desh Bachao was the central theme of Indira Gandhi’s 1971 election campaign, focusing on poverty eradication.
  • The slogan was formally integrated into the Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974–1979), which emphasized:
    • Direct poverty alleviation programs.
    • Employment generation schemes.
    • Reduction of income inequality.
  • This plan marked a shift from purely growth-oriented strategies to inclusive development, targeting the rural and urban poor.

question: The 'Two-Nation Theory' was formally propounded in the Lahore Session of the Muslim League in which year?
option_1: 1930
option_2: 1933
option_3: 1937
option_4: 1940
solution_text:
The 'Two-Nation Theory' was formally propounded in the Lahore Session of the Muslim League in 1940, during the adoption of the Lahore Resolution (also called the Pakistan Resolution).

Explanation

  • The Two-Nation Theory argued that Hindus and Muslims were two distinct nations, with separate religions, cultures, and traditions, and therefore could not coexist under a single nation.
  • It was first articulated by Muhammad Iqbal in his 1930 Allahabad Address, but it was formally adopted by the Muslim League in Lahore in March 1940 under the leadership of Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
  • The Lahore Resolution (23 March 1940) declared that Muslims in India should have their own independent states in regions where they were in majority.
  • This resolution laid the foundation for the creation of Pakistan in 1947.

question: Which of the following is subtracted from NNP (Net National Product) at market price to derive NNP at factor cost?
option_1: Depreciation
option_2: Net Indirect Taxes
option_3: Net Factor Income from Abroad
option_4: Net Direct Taxes
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Net Indirect Taxes

Explanation

  • Net National Product (NNP) at Market Price includes the effect of indirect taxes and subsidies.
  • To derive NNP at Factor Cost, we subtract Net Indirect Taxes (Indirect Taxes – Subsidies) from NNP at Market Price.
  • This is because Factor Cost reflects the income earned by factors of production (land, labor, capital, entrepreneurship), excluding the impact of government taxes and subsidies.

question: 'Solder' is an alloy consisting of which two metals?
option_1: Lead and Tin
option_2: Copper and Zinc
option_3: Iron and Carbon
option_4: Copper and Tin
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Lead and Tin

Explanation

  • Solder is a fusible metal alloy used to join together metal workpieces, especially in electronics and plumbing.
  • The most common traditional solder is made of Lead (Pb) and Tin (Sn).
  • It has a low melting point (around 180–190°C), which makes it ideal for creating strong joints without damaging components.
  • Modern solders often use lead-free alternatives (like tin-copper or tin-silver alloys) due to health and environmental concerns, but the classic composition remains Lead + Tin.

question: Where does the Pindar and Alaknanda rivers meet in Uttarakhand?
option_1: Vishnuprayag
option_2: Karnaprayag
option_3: Devaprayag
option_4: Rudraprayag
solution_text:
Correct Answer: (b) Karnaprayag

Explanation:

  • The Pindar River meets the Alaknanda River at Karnaprayag in Uttarakhand.
  • Karnaprayag is one of the Panch Prayag (five confluences) of the Alaknanda River.
  • The Pindar originates from the Pindari Glacier in the Kumaon Himalayas and flows westward to join Alaknanda.
  • This confluence holds both geographical and religious significance in the Himalayan river system.

question: In which among the following movements, Gandhiji first used the Hunger Strike?

option_3: Ahmedabad

solution_text:
Correct Answer: (c) Ahmedabad

Explanation:

  • Gandhi undertook a fast unto death to ensure justice for workers and to maintain discipline among them.
  • This was the first time he used fasting as a moral pressure tool, which later became a key method in his movements.

question:
Instruction:
Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option.

Statement:
With the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence and automation technologies, many traditional jobs across industries such as manufacturing, customer service, and even professional sectors are increasingly being replaced or transformed. While these innovations improve efficiency and productivity, they also raise serious concerns about large-scale unemployment, widening income inequality, and the need for reskilling the workforce. Therefore, governments and organizations must proactively invest in skill development programs and establish regulatory frameworks to ensure a balanced and inclusive economic transition.

Assumptions:
I. Automation and artificial intelligence can significantly impact employment levels across various sectors.
II. Skill development programs can help workers adapt to technological changes.
III. Economic transition due to automation can lead to inequality if not managed properly.
option_1: Only I and II are implicit

solution_text:

  • It also recommends skill development programs, implying they are effective → Assumption II is implicit.
  • The mention of income inequality concerns indicates that imbalance may occur → Assumption III is also implicit.
  • All three assumptions logically support the statement.

question:
Instruction:
In this question some conditions or circumstances are provided to you, you have to make logical judgement according to mentioned circumstances and mark the correct option.

option_1: All engineers are employed

option_3: All skilled professionals are engineers
option_4: Some engineers are unemployed
solution_text:
Explanation:

  • From statement 1: All engineers ⟶ skilled professionals
  • From statement 2: No skilled professionals ⟶ unemployed (i.e., all skilled professionals are employed)
  • Therefore, engineers (being skilled professionals) cannot be unemployed
  • Hence, all engineers are employed

Explanation:

  • (b) Incorrect → engineers cannot be unemployed
  • (c) Incorrect → reverse of statement, not valid
  • (d) Incorrect → directly contradicts the given statements

question: If 5th March 2026 is a Wednesday, what day will 18th March 2026 be?
option_1: Wednesday
option_2: Thursday
option_3: Tuesday
option_4: Saturday
solution_text:
Explanation:

  • Difference between 5th March and 18th March = 13 days
  • 13 ÷ 7 = 1 week + 6 days
  • So, move 6 days ahead from Wednesday
  • Wednesday + 6 days = Tuesday
